title: FINAL FANTASY IX

notes:
Using steam-native on Arch Linux, running FF9.exe in the x64 folder.

XInput gamepad works perfectly

Alt-tab does not cause issues.

Tested from New Game to the Evil Forest Active Time Event. No issues.

Set launch options to:
cd ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/FINAL\ FANTASY\ IX/; STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377840/pfx ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.7\ Beta/proton run x64/FF9.exe

title: FINAL FANTASY IX

title: FINAL FANTASY IX

notes:
Using workaround published in a report from other user here. It works with Xbox One Controller. I am using latest NVIDIA driver (410.73) with GTX 1060 6GB on Ubuntu 18.04 with KDE desktop.  
You have to add launch options like this: cd /INSTALLATION_PATH/steamapps/common/FINAL\ FANTASY\ IX/; STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH=/INSTALLATION_PATH/steamapps/compatdata/377840/pfx /INSTALLATION_PATH/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.7\ Beta/proton run x64/FF9.exe
title: FINAL FANTASY IX

notes:
1. Run & install prerequirements.
2. Righ clic on game on the list, select properties.
3. Select "Launch parameters"
4. Insert "cd ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/FINAL\ FANTASY\ IX/; STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377840/pfx ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.7\/proton run x64/FF9.exe"

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=es_I_kbluok
title: FINAL FANTASY IX

notes: 1. Ran game without any adjustments. After installation of dependencies, game attempted to run, but immediately exits. 2. Added "cd ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/FINAL\ FANTASY\ IX/; STEAM_COMPAT_DATA_PATH=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/377840/pfx ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 3.7\/proton run x64/FF9.exe" to 'Launch Options', as suggested in other reports, and game seems to be running perfectly.
